name: Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: In a mixing bowl combine dry milk powder, powdered sugar, creamer, and cocoa.
Store in airtight container.
For each serving, place 1/3 cup of the mix in a mug and add 3/4 cup boiling  water.
NOTES : Mocha Mix: Prepare as above, except stir 1/2 cup instant coffee crystals into mix.

name: Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: Mix ingredients together and mix well.
Use 1/3 Cup of mixture per cup of boiling water.

name: Swiss Mocha Mix
directions: Measure all of the ingredients into a clean dry bowl.
Mix them all together really well with a fork or a wire whisk.
It's important to get everything evenly distributed for the best flavor.
When it is all mixed up, transfer the mixture to resealable container or a pretty jar, or a zip lock bag.
Store it on the pantry shelf.
To Prepare: Measure 3 or 4 tablespoons into a coffee cup.
Fill it up with hot water (about 3/4 cup) and stir to dissolve.

name: Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients in a large mixing pot.
Fill a cup or mug half way with mix.
Add boiling hot water to mug or cup.
Stir and enjoy.

name: Homemade Chai Mix
directions: Mix one cup of powdered dry milk with the brown sugar and spices and blend in the blender or food processor, to get the lumps out of the brown sugar.
Combine with remaining ingredients and mix well.
The turmeric adds a rich, lovely color to the finished brew.
The white pepper adds a nice warmth.
Start with two rounded tablespoons of mix to an 8 ounce cup of hot water; stir and enjoy.
Store mix in a covered container.

name: Instant Hot Chocolate
directions: Mix all ingredients and store in air tight container.
I use the gallon size ice cream plastic container to store.
Use 1/4 cup mix with 3/4 cup hot water.
Add marshmallows or whatever topping you normally would to hot chocolate.
Okay, I'm not the math whiz and I'm guessing on the servings, all I know it makes a lot.

name: Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Sift all the ingredients into an air tight container.
Attach to container: 1 serving= 4 heaping tablespoons hot cocoa mix, 8 ounces boiling water.

name: Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: Mix all this together.
Store in container.
When ready to use fill mug or cup with half mix and then fill with hot water.

name: Instant Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together and store in a air tight container.
To use: Mix 1/3 cup mix with 3/4 to 1 cup hot water for each cup of hot chocolate.

name: Hot Chocolate Mix Supreme
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Store in an airtight container in a dry cool place.
Use 1/3 mix to 2/3 very hot or boiling water or to taste.
**NOTE**the amounts may be doubled or scaled down to suit needs.

name: Instant Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together and store in an airtight container.
To make, fill cup 1/4 to 1/2 full with mix and pour in hot water or warm milk.

name: Whole Foods Hot Chocolate Mix (with 3 Options)
directions: Mix all the ingredients in the Traditional cocoa mix.
I give everything a whizz in the food processor to combine well.
Store in an airtight container.
Spiced cocoa mix: To one recipe of the traditional mix, add the spices and mix.
Enjoy a warming spiced cocoa!
Java Cocoa: To one recipe of traditional mix, mix in instant coffee powder.
To make hot cocoa, place 1/4 cup of mix in a mug.
Stir in 1 cup of boiling hot water.
Hot mint Cocoa: Steep one mint tea bag in a cup of hot traditional cocoa.

name: Hot Chocolate Drink Mix
directions: Mix the ingredients.
Store in a covered container in a dry place.
To make hot chocolate:.
Mix 3 heaping teaspoons of mixture in a mix and add boiling water.  Top with mini marshmallows if desired.

name: Snickerdoodle Coffee Mix
directions: Combine all the ingredients together; store in airtight containers.
Instructions: add 3 tablespoons coffee mix to 3/4 cup boiling water; stir to dissolve.

name: Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Using a sifter, mix cocoa, sugar, salt, cornstarch & pepper.
Stir in powdered milk.
Store in a tightly sealed container.  Will keep for several months.
To make a cup of hot cocoa, put 1/3 cup of mix in a mug.
Pour a small amount of boiling water into the mug and blend cocoa mix well.  Add up to 3/4 cup boiling water.  Stir well & serve.

name: Daddy's Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: Mix all this together.
Store in container.
When ready to use fill mug or cup with half mix and then fill with hot water.

name: Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together thoroughly and store in an airtight container.
When ready to serve place 1/3 cup of mix in a 8-10 ounce mug and pour about 3/4 cup of boiling water over the mix; stir to dissolve.
Serve with a candy cane or tiny marshmallows.

name: Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Whisk all together in a large bowl.
Keep in airtight container.
Place 1/4 cup mix in a cocoa mug and fill with boiling water.
Again, adjust this amount to your tastes.

name: Instant Mexican Style Hot Cocoa
directions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing bowl. Stir slowly to combine.
Sift mixture into your storage container. DO NOT skip this step. Sifting helps mix the ingredients, aerate, and break up the clumps.
Store in a sealed container and be sure to give it a good shake before you use any of the mix. You can adjust the amount of cayenne pepper to taste as needed.
To use mix.
Fill a mug about half full with the mix and then slowly top it off with hot water, stirring the whole time. Use more or less to taste.

similar.name: Whole Foods Hot Chocolate Mix (with 3 Options)

name: Snickerdoodle Coffee Mix
directions: Combine ingredients; store in an airtight container.
To mix, use 4 large heaping teaspoonsfull per 6-8 oz. of boiling water.

name: Peanut Butter Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: Combine ingredients in a large bowl.
working in tow batches, pulse ingredients in food processor until chips are finely ground.
Store in air tight container for up to 3 months.
To Make Peanut Butter Hot Cocoa:
Stir 1/3 cup of this mix into 1 cup HOT MILK.
Top with whipped cream or marshmallows.
Raspberry Variation:

Use white chocolate chips in place of peanut butter chips, add 1 (3 oz) box raspberry gelatin and reduce confectioner's sugar to 1 1/2 cups. (NOTE: Be certain to use white chocolate chips as the white chocolate will provide a creamy texture).

name: Hot Chocolate
directions: Combine sugar and cocoa; add milk and a dash of salt.
For 1 serving place 1/3 cup dry mixture in a coffee mug, add boiling water and stir.

name: Zippy Hot Chocolate
directions: Mix all together in a very large mixing bowl.
Store in an airtight contaoner.
Use 3 heaping tablespoons of the mix with one cup boiling water.
Garnish with mini-marshmallows, if desired!

name: Gourmet Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Sift together all dry ingredients and store in covered container of your choosing.
To use mix: stir 2 heaping tablespoons of cocoa mix into 8 oz. mug of hot water.

name: Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Combine all the ingredients in a large bowl with a wire whisk, stirring until everything is well distributed.
Store in an air-tight container in a cool, dry place.
To serve, mix a heaping 1/4 cup of the mix into 6 to 8 ounces of very hot water.
Top with whipped cream or mini-marshmallows.
Obviously, this recipe is easily doubled.
For a crowd, 4 heaping cups of this mix will make about a gallon of hot chocolate.

name: Mexican Cocoa Mix
directions: In a large bowl, mix together nonfat dry milk, malted milk powder, chocolate milk powder, cocoa powder, sugar and cinnamon. Sift into another large bowl. Sift again into the first bowl.
Spoon mixture into a 1-quart canning jar. Gently tap on counter a few times to settle ingredients.
Place lid on jar and decorate as desired.
To present as a gift, include in a basket with a bottle of coffee-flavored liqueur, cinnamon sticks and chocolates, if desired. Attach a gift tag with the instructions for preparing by the cup.
Cocoa By The Cup: Place 1/3 cup of mix in a coffee mug. Pour 8 ounces boiling water into mug and stir.

name: Flavored Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together. Sift thoroughly for best results. Use 6 Tablespoons  per mug. You can use hot water or milk. It is plenty creamy with just water!

name: Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients. Pack into airtight containers.
To server: Place 4 heaping tablespoons mix inot a mug, add 1 cup hot water and stir.  Add marshmallows or whipped cream.

similar.name: Hot Cocoa- Just Add Boiling Water

name: Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing bowl and incorporate evenly. In a small pot, heat 4 to 6 cups of water.
Fill your mug half full with the mixture and pour in hot water. Stir to combine. Seal the rest in an airtight container, keeps indefinitely in the pantry. This also works great with warm milk.

name: Best Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: Mix dry ingredients. Store in a cool, dry location. To make use about 1/3 C mixture for each cup of hot water (or milk).

name: Hot Cocoa- Just Add Boiling Water
directions: Makes a very large quantity! Mix all ingredients together very well and then use a sifter and sift everything together.

name: Hot Cocoa (Aka Snowballs in Cocoa)
directions: Mix well and fill canning jar.
Label: Fill your mug half full with the mixture and pour in hot water. Stir to combine. Seal the rest in an airtight container, keeps indefinitely in the pantry. This also works great with warm milk.

similar.name: Whole Foods Hot Chocolate Mix (with 3 Options)

name: Coffee Creamer
directions: Mix first three ingredients in blender until creamy.  You can adjust the water to your preference.  Add a few drops of coffee or food flavoring as noted below.
*To date I have used about 1/4 tsp hazelnut, or 1/4 tsp hazelnut with 1 Tbsp cocoa powder.  Both have been excellent.  I plan on trying peppermint, pumpkin,
*  I have also frozen strong coffee in a container then whirled it along with this creamer for an excellent iced coffee.
Don't be afraid to make your own flavors to fit your tastes or even the seasons.

similar.name: Hot Cocoa- Just Add Boiling Water

name: Sugar Free Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together in food processor.
I use 4 tablespoons in 8 oz boiling water.
Can add a few mini marshmallows or Cool Whip on top, with a little sprinkle of cinnamon.

similar.name: Alton Brown Hot Cocoa Mix (Can Be Dairy-Free)

name: Jayme's Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Mix all the ingredients together in a large container.
Mix well.
Then sift the mixture into container to make the granules finer.
Keep in a sealed container.
Mix 3 heaping tablespoons of mix (about 1/3.
cup) to one cup boiIing water.
(You can vary the amount of mix added to the water.
depending on how much chocolate you want.
The original recipe says 2 heaping.
tablespoons.( I LIKE CHOCOLATEll).

name: Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together in large bowl.
Place in jars & label.
To make a cup of cocoa, place 1/3 cup of mix in large mug and fill with boiling water.
Enjoy.

name: Home Made Hot Chocolate
directions: combine and mix 2 heaping Tbsp in 8oz hot water.

name: Malted Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: In a large mixing bowl, combine all the ingredients until evenly mixed. Store in an airtight container.
To serve: place 2 to 3 Tablespoons of the cocoa mix in a 6-ounce mug. Add boiling water. Stir to combine. Serve hot.

similar.name: Hot Cocoa- Just Add Boiling Water

name: Alton Brown Hot Cocoa Mix (Can Be Dairy-Free)
directions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing bowl and incorporate evenly. Seal in airtight container till ready to use.
Warm water or dairy-alternative.

Alton's directions at the above-mentioned web address state to fill mug half-full with mix and add liquid. I can't do dairy so my experience is without the bulk of the pwd milk. I put 3 T in my mug, add a little of my warmed coconut milk and mix well. Add a bit more coconut milk, mix, and continue until filled and thoroughly incorporated.
(Somebody else has posted his homemade marshmallows on food.com if you're so inclined. I haven't tried them yet.).
